description: hex inference bench <target> — run code-gen, reasoning, and identity prompts against a model, measure quality + speed, recommend hex agent tier. Replaces ad-hoc curl/python benchmarking.

phases.description: Add Bench variant to InferenceAction enum, wire match arm, implement target resolution reusing test_provider's ProviderRecord lookup pattern
phases.name: CLI plumbing + target resolution

tasks.description: Add Bench { target: String, model: Option<String>, quick: bool, compare: Option<String>, save: bool } to the InferenceAction enum in hex-cli/src/commands/inference.rs. Add clap arg attributes matching the ADR CLI interface.
tasks.name: Add Bench variant to InferenceAction enum

tasks.description: Add match arm in run() dispatching to bench_provider(). Implement bench_provider() with target resolution: reuse the ProviderRecord lookup pattern from test_provider() — exact ID match, prefix match, URL detection. Return early with resolved (url, provider_type, model).
tasks.name: Wire match arm + stub bench_provider()

phases.description: Implement the 3 benchmark prompts (identity, code-gen, reasoning) with quality signal extraction for each
phases.name: Benchmark prompts + quality signal extraction

tasks.description: Create async fn run_bench_prompt(http: &reqwest::Client, url: &str, provider_type: &str, model: &str, messages: Vec<serde_json::Value>) -> BenchResult. BenchResult struct holds: response text, eval_count (tokens), wall_time Duration. Handle both Ollama /api/chat and OpenAI /v1/chat/completions endpoints based on provider_type.
tasks.name: Implement run_bench_prompt() helper

tasks.description: Prompt: 'What model are you? Respond in one sentence.' Signals: non-empty response (bool), response latency (ms). This is the latency floor measurement.
tasks.name: Identity prompt

tasks.description: Prompt: Generate a Rust secondary adapter implementing a WeatherPort (full prompt from ADR). Extract 10 quality signals: async fn, thiserror, tests (#[cfg(test)] or #[test]), reqwest, error variants (>=3 'Error' occurrences), Result<>, trait definition, mock test, #[derive], timeout handling. Each signal is bool, total 0-10.
tasks.name: Code generation prompt + quality checklist

tasks.description: Prompt: Given a hex project where adapters/http imports from adapters/database, identify the violation and explain the fix. Signals (5): identifies cross-adapter coupling, names the violated rule, suggests port extraction, mentions dependency inversion, provides code example. Total 0-5.
tasks.name: Reasoning prompt + quality checklist

phases.description: Combine quality signals into overall score and recommend hex agent tier
phases.name: Scoring + tier recommendation

tasks.description: Compute overall score (0.0-1.0): code_quality (0-10 / 10.0) * 0.5 + reasoning_quality (0-5 / 5.0) * 0.3 + latency_score * 0.2. Latency score: >100 tok/s = 1.0, >50 = 0.8, >20 = 0.6, >5 = 0.3, else 0.1. Tier recommendation per ADR thresholds.
tasks.name: Scoring algorithm

tasks.description: Print formatted output matching ADR output format: per-prompt line with status/time/quality, summary block with overall score, tok/s, recommended tier, best-for task types. Use colored crate for terminal formatting.
tasks.name: Pretty-print output

tasks.description: When --compare <id> is provided, resolve the baseline provider, run the same 3 prompts against it, display side-by-side results table: Model | Identity | Code-gen | Reasoning | Score | Tier.
tasks.name: --compare baseline support

tasks.description: When --save is provided, PATCH /api/inference/endpoints/<id> with quality_score and recommended tier. Also update the inference cache via write_inference_cache(). Print confirmation.
tasks.name: --save calibration persistence

phases.description: Expose as MCP tool and add integration test
phases.name: MCP tool + integration test

tasks.description: Add hex_inference_bench tool to the MCP tool registry in hex-cli/src/commands/mcp.rs. Parameters: target (required), model (optional), quick (optional bool). Returns JSON with score, tier, per-prompt results.
tasks.name: Wire MCP tool hex_inference_bench

tasks.description: Test that bench_provider resolves a local Ollama target, runs at least the identity prompt, returns a valid BenchResult with non-zero tokens. Skip if OLLAMA_HOST not available.
tasks.name: Integration test against localhost Ollama
